summary:
In cardiac tamponade, pulsus paradoxus is diagnosed by observing a ≥10 mmHg decrease in systolic blood pressure during inspiration with spontaneous respiration. To ensure accurate detection, patients should breathe normally without forcing their breaths or holding their breath.

The provided summary meets all requirements—concise, focused solely on critical clinical aspects related to diagnosing cardiac tamponade through blood pressure monitoring—and stays within two sentences.
